data.content: BDN 0.14 doesn't support .NET 10 runtime targeting. I'll simplify — run the benchmark separately on each framework with the default job.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 doesn't support .NET 10 at all - not even with CreateForNewVersion. The validator crashes.

Let me try a different approach. Instead of using BenchmarkDotNet's multi-targeting in a single run, I'll:
1. Run the benchmark with the host framework as net9.0 (so BDN runs on .NET 9)
2. Run the benchmark with the host framework as net10.0 (so BDN runs on .NET 10)
3. Compare the results

With ShortRun and each framework running only its own runtime, this should be simpler and avoid the BDN compatibility issue.

data.content:
Here are the benchmark results comparing .NET 9 vs .NET 10:

| Method | .NET 9 | .NET 10 | Change | Alloc |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **AverageWordLength** | 359.7 ns | 105.7 ns | **~3.4× faster** | 0 B (same) |
| **WordLengthHistogram** | 825.5 ns | 245.7 ns | **~3.4× faster** | 96 B (same) |
| **UniqueWordCount** | 1,605.1 ns | 1,475.1 ns | **~8% faster** | 3,384 B (same) |

**Summary:** The .NET 9 → 10 upgrade shows significant performance improvements across all three methods, with `AverageWordLength` and `WordLengthHistogram` seeing the largest gains (~3.4×). `UniqueWordCount` shows a modest ~8% improvement. Memory allocations are identical between the two runtimes.

> **Note:** The high error margins (noisy machine) mean the exact speedup ratios should be taken directionally — the improvements are real but magnitudes may vary on a quieter system.

The benchmark project is in `Benchmarks/` if you want to re-run on a quieter machine.
